decks.notes: The deck focuses on a mixture of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells aimed at controlling the opponent's board while pressuring their life total. By utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, the deck aims to establish an early board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game. Cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Quantum Riddler provide additional disruption and control elements, allowing the deck to dictate the pace of the game and outmaneuver the opponent. The deck aims to quickly close out games before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The deck focuses on quickly cycling through cards in order to fill up the graveyard with creatures, then casting Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a sudden influx of creatures and take control of the game. The deck includes various cards that enable cycling, as well as creatures that benefit from being brought back with Living End. It also includes cards to protect the combo, disrupt the opponent's strategy, and provide additional value.

The deck focuses on aggressively deploying fast and powerful creatures, utilizing burn spells and combat tricks to control the board and overwhelm the opponent. Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer provide strong early game pressure, while Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury and Voice of Victory offer powerful late game finishers. Ranger-Captain of Eos and Seasoned Pyromancer provide card advantage, while Goblin Bombardment and Galvanic Discharge serve as flexible removal options. The deck aims to quickly close out games and punish slow or greedy opponents.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays, utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls to quickly pressure the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm to finish off the opponent or clear blockers. The deck aims to maintain board presence through cards like Seasoned Pyromancer and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury. Additionally, support cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Arena of Glory help to boost the team's power and resilience, allowing for a fast and relentless assault on the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with Birthing Ritual and utilizing cards like Magmatic Hellkite, Omnath, Locus of Creation, Shardless Agent, and Bloodbraid Elf to put pressure on the opponent. Leyline Binding and Subtlety help control the board while Quantum Riddler provides card advantage. The deck's mana base is flexible with fetch lands and various shock lands to support a 4/5 color Aggro strategy. Overall, the key strategy is to deploy threats quickly and efficiently, leveraging the deck's powerful creatures and mana ramp to overwhelm the opponent.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride to quickly deal damage to the opponent. It also includes burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment to control the board and finish off the opponent. The deck's key strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and removal spells, while also benefiting from cards like Guide of Souls and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to gain advantage and maintain pressure on the opponent.

The key concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly establish a strong board presence with efficient and aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Voice of Victory, backed up by burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment. The deck aims to apply pressure to the opponent early on and maintain that pressure throughout the game, utilizing cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Seasoned Pyromancer to generate additional threats. The inclusion of disruptive cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Blood Moon helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan and secure victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through a combination of hand disruption and counter spells. By using cards like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, the deck can control the board and prevent the opponent from advancing their game plan. Cards like Orcish Bowmasters and Psychic Frog provide additional control elements, while Kaito, Bane of Nightmares offers a win condition by punishing the opponent for their actions. With a mix of removal, counterspells, and disruptive creatures, this deck aims to slow down the opponent and take control of the game.

This deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana using Birthing Ritual and utilizing powerful low-cost creatures to apply pressure on the opponent. With a mix of disruptive counter spells like Flare of Denial and Sink into Stupor, the deck aims to control the game while maintaining a steady stream of threats. The inclusion of creatures like Subtlety and Abhorrent Oculus provide additional disruption and evasion, allowing the deck to close out games quickly. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm and outpace the opponent, generating strong board presence and aggression early on. By using cards like Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble to generate additional value and control the board with cards like Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke, the deck aims to quickly close out games with strong artifact creatures like Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer. The inclusion of cards like Shadowspear and Claws of Gix provide flexibility and additional utility in disrupting the opponent's game plan.

The key strategy of this deck is to utilize Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Mox Amber to generate mana and create infinite combos with Grinding Station, Mishra's Bauble, and Urza's Saga. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Spellskite and Metallic Rebuke to control the game state. Additionally, the deck incorporates unconventional cards like Sewer-veillance Cam and Does Machines to further disrupt the opponent's strategy and advance the combo win condition. Overall, this deck aims to combo off quickly and efficiently while disrupting the opponent's plans to secure a win in a Modern 1 vs 1 matchup.

The deck focuses on using Allosaurus Rider and Neoform to quickly assemble the combo with Ghalta, Stampede Tyrant or Griselbrand to finish off the opponent. The deck also has additional combo enablers like Eldritch Evolution and Generous Ent, as well as protection spells like Pact of Negation and Veil of Summer. The deck aims to ramp up mana quickly with cards like Hedge Maze and Planar Genesis, while also using Atraxa, Grand Unifier to provide additional value and synergy. Ultimately, the deck's goal is to execute its combo pieces efficiently to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on cycling through cards to fill the graveyard with creatures, then casting Living End to bring them all back onto the battlefield for a powerful combo finish. It includes cards like Curator of Mysteries and Street Wraith for cycling, as well as Endurance and Generous Ent to protect the combo. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Negation to protect the combo and disrupt opponent's strategies. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a board full of powerful creatures after casting Living End.

This deck focuses on playing aggressive creatures while disrupting opponent's game plan with taxing effects. It also has access to removal spells and counterspells to control the board. The key strategy is to pressure the opponent with quick threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while using cards like Quantum Riddler and Teferi, Time Raveler to slow down the opponent's game plan. Consign to Memory and Solitude can help deal with threats efficiently, giving the deck an edge in the early game and maintaining control throughout the match.

The main concept of this Mono Green Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and pump spells. The deck focuses on mana acceleration with cards like Noble Hierarch and Green Sun's Zenith to get big threats like Old-Growth Troll and Steel Leaf Champion out early. The deck also includes removal like Dismember and Vines of Vastwood to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the goal is to put pressure on the opponent's life total and win the game quickly through combat damage.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game through the use of Leyline cards and removal spells while ramping up mana to eventually cast Scapeshift and trigger Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle for massive amounts of damage. The deck focuses on generating value from cards like Scion of Draco and Phlage, Titan of Fire's Fury while also maintaining a strong board presence with creatures like Ashling, Rekindled and Icetill Explorer. Overall, the goal is to outlast the opponent and finish them off with a powerful Scapeshift combo.
